Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $208,545,589Details
International Box Office $502,098,977
Worldwide Box Office $710,644,566
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $24,189,930
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $19,157,425
Total $43,347,355
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A growing nation of genetically evolved apes led by Caesar is threatened by a band of human survivors of the devastating virus unleashed a decade earlier. They reach a fragile peace, but it proves short-lived, as both sides are brought to the brink of a war that will determine who will emerge as Earth’s dominant species.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$72,611,427 (34.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.87 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:29.3% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$170,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.2 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,967 opening theaters/3,969 max. theaters, 5.5 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $305,288,281

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 11th, 2014 (Wide) by 20th Century Fox
International Releases: July 9th, 2014 (Wide) (Australia)
July 9th, 2014 (Wide) (Indonesia)
July 9th, 2014 (Wide) (Philippines)
July 10th, 2014 (Wide) (Israel)
July 10th, 2014 (Wide) (Lebanon)
Video Release: December 2nd, 2014 by Fox Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of sci-fi violence and action, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2327, 6/17/2014)
Running Time: 130 minutes
Franchise: Planet of the Apes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Motion Capture Performance, Remake
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Geographical setting: San Francisco
Plot point: Animals Gone Bad, War
Social setting: Post Apocalypse
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Time period setting: 2020s
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Animation/Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Chernin Entertainment, TSG Entertainment, Ingenious Media
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A growing nation of genetically evolved apes led by Caesar is threatened by a band of human survivors of the devastating virus unleashed a decade earlier. They reach a fragile peace, but it proves short-lived, as both sides are brought to the brink of a war that will determine who will emerge as Earth’s dominant species.
